lesson_title: Order of construction of derived classes

cards.front: When a derived class object is constructed, which part is constructed first: the base portion or the derived portion?
cards.back: The base portion is constructed first, then the derived portion.
cards.source: 24.3 - Order of construction of derived classes

cards.text: When C++ constructs derived objects, it constructs from {{c1::most-base}} to {{c2::most-derived}}. Destruction happens in {{c3::reverse}} order.
cards.source: 24.3 - Order of construction of derived classes

cards.question: What does this code output?
cards.explanation: The Base constructor is called first, printing "Base", then the Derived constructor prints "Derived".
cards.source: 24.3 - Order of construction of derived classes

cards.front: Why does C++ construct the base class portion before the derived class portion?
cards.back: Because the child class often uses variables and functions from the parent. Constructing the parent first ensures those variables are initialized before the derived class needs them.
cards.source: 24.3 - Order of construction of derived classes

cards.text: A derived class is actually a {{c1::two part}} class: one part {{c2::Base}}, and one part {{c2::Derived}}.
cards.source: 24.3 - Order of construction of derived classes

cards.question: What does this code output?
cards.explanation: In an inheritance chain, construction starts at the most-base class (A), then walks down the inheritance tree (B), ending at the most-derived class (C).
cards.source: 24.3 - Order of construction of derived classes

cards.text: When a derived class is destroyed, each destructor is called in {{c1::reverse}} order of construction: from {{c2::most-derived}} to {{c2::most-base}}.
cards.source: 24.3 - Order of construction of derived classes

cards.front: Are base class members copied into the derived class during construction?
cards.back: No, they are NOT copied. The derived class contains a base class portion and a derived class portion as separate parts.
cards.source: 24.3 - Order of construction of derived classes

cards.explanation:
C++ constructs derived classes in phases:
1. Most-base class is constructed
2. Each child class is constructed in order
3. Most-child class is constructed last
examples: class D: public C: public B: public A → Constructs A, B, C, D
cards.source: 24.3 - Order of construction of derived classes

cards.text: A child cannot exist without a {{c1::parent}}, which is why the parent class must be constructed {{c2::first}}.
cards.source: 24.3 - Order of construction of derived classes

cards.front: Which constructor is called for the base portion if not explicitly specified in the derived constructor?
cards.back: The default base class constructor is called.
cards.source: 24.3 - Order of construction of derived classes

cards.text: As each class in an inheritance chain is constructed, the {{c1::appropriate constructor}} from that class is called to {{c2::initialize that part}} of the class.
cards.source: 24.3 - Order of construction of derived classes
